title: RFE: podman machine with hyperkit (on MacOS)
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

I'd love to see podman machine supporting hyperkit as an alternative to qemu on MacOS.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init && podman machine start`


**Describe the results you received:**
 uses on MacOS in the background quemu

**Describe the results you expected:**
I'd love to have an option to use hyperkit (as rumors say, that it should be more performant)

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
I'm running MacOS

comments.body: Do we have any actual data on relative performance? Per my understanding, both of them are using the OS X hypervisor APIs, so I would expect performance to be about the same given the shared backend.

@typekpb can you substantiate the claim?  our machine code is heavily interfaced so it could be a possibility to be able to do it.  Hyperkit would also need the ability to shove the ignition file into the vm as well.

I think Docker are deprecating HyperKit for an internal implementation, talking to the Apple frameworks

These are the same Hypervisor.framework and Virtualization.framework that QEMU uses

https://www.docker.com/blog/the-magic-behind-the-scenes-of-docker-desktop/

> On Mac (on Intel and M1 machines) we are currently transitioning away from our previous HyperKit implementation to use Apple’s new Virtualization framework to run this VM
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have had no response and I do not see us doing this in the short time, I am closing.
